content:
- User asked whether complete removal of `parse_pg_conninfo` was already planned, or whether its continued presence came from archived tasks.
- Read current task/planning artifacts and progress history. Confirmed there is an explicit current task `.ralph/tasks/story-managed-start-intent-architecture/task-remove-managed-conf-parseback-and-rederive-start-intent.md` whose acceptance criteria say production parse-back should be removed unless a separate external-ingest boundary justifies keeping a parser.
- Also confirmed earlier authoritative-managed-config work intentionally kept parse-back for resume/rejoin by reading managed `pgtm.postgresql.conf` back into typed state; later progress entries show `parse_pg_conninfo` was promoted from test-only into production specifically to support that path in HA/runtime startup.
- Conclusion to communicate: yes, complete removal is now explicitly planned, but no, older implementation history did intentionally preserve it for managed start-intent reconstruction rather than archive it away.

- Read prior Choose-Task progress and inspected .ralph/current_tasks.md plus .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Reviewed every active task file for priority and blockers. There were no active bug tasks in current_tasks, no completed blockers listed in current_tasks_done, and no explicit <blocked_by> tags on the active task definitions.
- Selected the highest-precedence unblocked failing task: the in-progress high-priority docs chain entry .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md. Wrote that exact path to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- If resuming later, no further work is needed for this turn because the instruction was to quit immediately after writing the chosen task path.

- Read startup progress log and .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Current task points to .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md.
- Next step is to inspect that task file and determine whether I should write the first plan, review a TO BE VERIFIED plan, or execute an approved NOW EXECUTE plan.

- Opened the active task file and found it already at NOW EXECUTE.
- Task is docs-only and explicitly forbids tests; I will follow the k2-docs-loop workflow and use ask-k2 for the required K2 lanes.
- Next step is to read just the relevant skill instructions and then execute the next five-page batch or final blind review depending on current docs coverage.

- Inspected docs/src coverage at startup of this execution pass.
- Tutorial and likely other quadrants are still too thin to call the corpus complete, so passes stays false and another five-page batch is required.
- Creating isolated batch directories and checking the k2-docs-loop script shape before launching five parallel choose-doc lanes.

- Ran five parallel choose-doc lanes for batch 2.
- K2 selected four unique pages plus a duplicate debug-api reference page, so I am replacing the duplicate with a fresh choose-doc lane to preserve five independent targets.
- Current unique targets pending the replacement lane: reference/debug-api, reference/ha-decisions, how-to/monitor-via-metrics, how-to/handle-network-partition.

- First-pass K2 drafts are back.
- I found unsupported examples and invented fields in at least monitor-via-metrics, handle-network-partition, and add-cluster-node, so I am doing a factual-only correction pass before the required revision prompt.
- Next step is to mark unsupported claims with // todo in the draft texts and then send each corrected raw draft through the revision prompt.

- Executed batch 2 of the docs-only k2-docs-loop task under NOW EXECUTE.
- Startup coverage check still found the docs corpus incomplete because tutorial coverage remains too thin, so passes stayed false and I ran another five-lane batch.
- Ran five choose-doc lanes, replaced one duplicate debug-api target, then prepared five drafts with per-page verbose extra-context notes.
- Batch 2 published these pages into docs/src: reference/debug-api, reference/ha-decisions, how-to/monitor-via-metrics, how-to/handle-network-partition, how-to/add-cluster-node.
- K2 again produced tool-call artifacts in some revision outputs, so I used the gathered source-backed context plus the usable K2 structure and did minimal factual cleanup to publish clean docs pages.
- Updated docs/src/SUMMARY.md and the task file with batch 2 notes; marked explanation/how-to/reference as sufficiently populated for now, but tutorial is still not enough, so passes remains false.
- Did not run any tests or code-oriented validation commands because this docs-only task explicitly forbids them.
- Next startup should re-check startup coverage; if tutorial is still thin, run another five-page batch. If all quadrants look complete, do the final blind docs-only ask-k2 review before passes can become true.

- Startup: read prior progress log and current task pointer.
- Current task still points to .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md.
- Reading the task file now to determine whether this turn is still planning, plan verification, or straight execution.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it is already at NOW EXECUTE.
- This is still the docs-only k2-docs-loop task; tests remain explicitly forbidden by the task file.
- Reading only the k2-docs-loop and ask-k2 skill instructions now so I can perform the next startup coverage gate and then execute the required branch.

- Loaded the k2-docs-loop and ask-k2 skill instructions.
- Running the startup docs coverage gate now using only docs/src contents, since that determines whether I execute another five-page batch or the final blind review.
- Expectation from the previous turn is that tutorial may still be too thin, but I am re-checking the current corpus before choosing the branch.

- Startup coverage gate result: tutorial is still too thin (2 pages), so the task stays on the five-page batch path and passes remains false.
- Reading the loop script just enough to preserve the existing five-lane execution pattern before launching new choose-doc requests.
- Next step is five independent choose-doc calls, with duplicate replacement if needed.

- Confirmed another batch is required and created a fresh batch directory docs/tmp/k2-batch/20260308-batch4.
- Launching five fully independent choose-doc lanes now, each writing to its own file for later duplicate detection and replacement.
- After these finish I will inspect the chosen targets, reroll any duplicates, then gather the exact requested files per lane.

- The five choose-doc lanes completed.
- Reading the lane outputs now to extract the chosen target pages and requested source/support files.
- If any duplicate targets appear, I will immediately reroll only those lanes to preserve five unique outputs for the batch.

- Read all five choose-doc outputs. The selected targets are unique: two tutorials, one how-to, one reference, one explanation.
- I am now gathering the exact requested source files plus repo searches for the extra factual questions before writing the required verbose per-page context notes.
- After the context notes exist, I will run the five prepare-draft lanes in parallel and then do factual-only cleanup on the returned drafts.

- Read only the requested single-node source files for a docs-source-gathering question.
- Extracted exact facts from docker-compose.single.yml, single node runtime.toml, src/bin/pgtuskmaster.rs, src/config/schema.rs, and tests/policy_e2e_api_only.rs.
- Next I am answering concisely with bullet points covering minimum PostgreSQL version evidence, parity vs multi-node behavior, ports/services, and essential versus optional runtime config sections for a minimal single-node tutorial.

- Gathered source-backed facts for docs questions on debug API usage and DCS state model.
- Read only the requested files plus narrowly scoped supporting sections in src/api/worker.rs and src/debug_api/view.rs to confirm since filtering, route availability, auth behavior, and endpoint listing.
- Ready to answer with concise factual bullets and exact file references; no edits or tests run.

- Read only the files requested for the docs-source questions: src/dcs/store.rs, src/dcs/keys.rs, src/ha/decide.rs, src/ha/decision.rs, src/ha/lower.rs, src/dcs/state.rs, src/ha/process_dispatch.rs, src/process/jobs.rs, tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs.
- Extracted the specific source facts for node-removal support, DCS key cleanup, HA phase transitions, decision lowering, process dispatch behavior, and the fail-safe vs recovery branches.
- Next useful step after this turn would be to patch the two docs pages using these exact source-backed bullets, keeping the wording explicit that node removal currently looks like external stop plus DCS observation rather than a dedicated graceful remove-node workflow.

- Wrote five verbose per-page context files under docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context using only the requested source files plus targeted repo facts.
- Launching the five prepare-draft lanes now for single-node tutorial, debug-api tutorial, remove-cluster-node how-to, dcs-state-model reference, and ha-decision-engine explanation.
- After the drafts return I need to do the mandated factual-only cleanup, add // todo markers where claims are unsupported, run the revision prompt pass, then minimally clean/publish the results.

- First prepare-draft launch failed because the context directory redirection path was created in parallel and lost the race.
- The batch directories now exist, and I relaunched all five prepare-draft lanes with the same inputs.
- No K2 draft work was lost beyond the failed shell redirections.

- All five first-pass prepare-draft lanes finished.
- Reading the generated draft markdown now to classify which drafts are usable and which contain tool-call artifacts or unsupported claims.
- Next required step is factual-only cleanup: insert // todo markers rather than rewriting unsupported content, then send each whole draft through the revision prompt.

- Inserted factual-only // todo markers into the five first-pass drafts where K2 invented commands, endpoints, or stronger claims than the requested sources supported.
- Launching the required raw revision prompt pass now for all five drafts.
- After these complete I need a minimal cleanup only: strip tool-call artifacts/placeholders that remain, add valid mermaid fences if the content supports them, then publish into docs/src and update SUMMARY/task notes.

- Published the five chosen batch pages into docs/src with source-backed content after the revision pass partially failed with tool-call artifacts.
- Updated docs/src/SUMMARY.md to include the new tutorial, how-to, explanation, and reference pages.
- Doing a final docs-only sanity pass now, then I need to update the task file with batch 4 notes, keep passes false for the next startup decision point, and commit/push because this task quits after each completed five-page batch.
